management_notes: Keith Neumeyer founded First Majestic in 2002 and serves as President & CEO — one of the most recognized silver mining executives globally with a long track record of building producing assets. Owns ~4.07M shares. Institutional ownership at 60%. Management has consistently delivered production growth and growing reserves, including a record 15.4M oz silver in 2025. Capital allocation has improved with dividend increases and a strong cash position of $940M post Q4 2025.
geology_notes: As of December 31, 2025: Proven & Probable reserves of 184.8M AgEq oz across 4 producing Mexican mines (27.4M tonnes). M&I resources of 652.8M AgEq oz (up 50% YoY) and Inferred 592.3M AgEq oz (up 69% YoY), driven by Santo Niño discovery at Santa Elena and Jerritt Canyon gains. Record resource growth in 2025 with reserves also growing 4% YoY. Four producing mines in Mexico; Jerritt Canyon (Nevada) in exploration/resource expansion mode.
capital_notes: ~494M shares outstanding (April 2026). Large liquid float is standard for a major silver producer. Cash of $940M as of end-Q4 2025 with no material debt concerns. Dividend-paying with quarterly distributions increased in 2025. Share count is large but proportional to asset base and operational scale. Market cap ~US$9.8B (NYSE, May 2026 at ~$19.76/share).
catalyst_notes: 2026 full-year production guidance issued; four producing mines provide continuous newsflow. Jerritt Canyon resource expansion drilling ongoing with results expected throughout 2026. Santo Niño maiden resource is a potential re-rating catalyst. High operating leverage to silver price — silver has rallied significantly in 2025-2026. Quarterly dividends and earnings releases are steady near-term catalysts.
acquisition_notes: Trading at ~US$19.76 (May 2026) with market cap ~$9.8B. Forward P/E of ~57x is above industry average (~21x), suggesting the market has priced in growth. Analyst consensus target ~$24.58-$28.35 USD implies upside of ~25-43% from current levels. Trades at a premium multiple appropriate for a proven silver producer with growing reserves, but the premium limits P/NAV value-hunting appeal versus smaller peers.
analyst_summary: First Majestic Silver is the highest-quality name in this cohort — a proven multi-mine silver producer with record 2025 production, a growing reserve and resource base, and $940M in cash. Keith Neumeyer's leadership and track record are a genuine differentiator. At a forward P/E above 57x, the stock is pricing in considerable silver price and production growth, limiting margin of safety for new buyers; it is a WATCH rather than a BUY at current multiples. For investors with high conviction on silver prices above $30/oz, AG offers excellent leverage with lower execution risk than junior peers.
reserve_probable: 184.8 million AgEq oz P&P reserves across 4 producing mines (Dec 31, 2025)
